spec_match.rationale: The text and code blocks accurately describe the syntax and capabilities of cross-origin and integrity modifiers.

interactive.rationale: The page is entirely static. It serves as a code card with no live controls, toggles, or actual resource-fetching simulations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent index states that 'the browser verifies the hash and rejects the resource', but there is no live demonstration of this behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: No edge cases or failure modes (such as a CORS failure or hash mismatch) are demonstrated or simulated on the page.

references.rationale: Includes a direct link to the target ChromeStatus entry in the see also section.

design_system.rationale: Follows standard design system guidelines, utilizing shared CSS variables and page layouts.

openQuestions.title: Transform page into a genuinely interactive CORS and SRI tester
openQuestions.detail: The demo currently violates the repository rule against static code cards. We should implement a live tester where the user can click a button to apply a background-image to an element with various modifier combinations (CORS mode, correct/incorrect SRI hash), and display a visual log indicating whether the request succeeded or was blocked by the browser.

openQuestions.title: Illustrate CORS credentials and fallback failures
openQuestions.detail: We should add a section showing a live comparison of loading a cross-origin image with cross-origin(anonymous) vs cross-origin(use-credentials) vs none, outputting the Network or HTTP response header expectations so users can see why a server might reject a request.

summary: This concept page is currently a static reference card rather than a genuinely interactive demo, which violates core repository rules. While the spec explanation and references are accurate, it requires a major rewrite to allow interactive CORS and SRI testing, featuring live resource loads, simulated or actual failures, and visual consoles.
